gen.prompt: Generate the corresponding C code based on the following description, method_signature, header_code, filename, and context. ## description In a monitoring system, format performance metrics for output. The function must format user-defined metric names, label key-value pairs, sampling intervals, and other information into standardized monitoring data points. Users can customize the lengths of metric names and labels, and the function must generate a standardized metric string. ## method_signature int format_metric_point(char* metric_name, char** label_keys, char** label_values, int label_count, char* metric_output) ## c standard C99 ## filename monitoring_system.c ## header_code ```c #ifndef MONITORING_SYSTEM_H #define MONITORING_SYSTEM_H #include #include #include #include #include /** * @brief Format performance metric data points for a monitoring system * * Formats the metric name and label key-value pairs into a standardized monitoring data point string. * The generated format is: metric_name{label_key1="label_value1",label_key2="label_value2",...} * * Input/output example: * Input: metric_name="http_requests_total", label_keys=["method","status"], label_values=["GET","200"], label_count=2, metric_output points to a sufficiently large buffer * Output: On success, returns the length of the formatted string; metric_output contains "http_requests_total{method=\"GET\",status=\"200\"}"; on failure, returns -1 * * @param metric_name The metric name string, e.g., "http_requests_total" * @param label_keys Array of label keys, each element pointing to a label key string, e.g., ["method", "status"] * @param label_values Array of label values, each element pointing to a label value string, corresponding one-to-one with label_keys, e.g., ["GET", "200"] * @param label_count Number of label key-value pairs, i.e., the length of the label_keys and label_values arrays * @param metric_output Output buffer to store the formatted metric string; the caller must ensure the buffer is large enough * * @return On success, returns the length of the formatted string (excluding the '\0' terminator); on failure, returns -1 * Failure conditions include: metric_name is NULL, label_count > 0 but label_keys or label_values is NULL, * metric_output is NULL, or an error occurs during formatting */ int format_metric_point(char* metric_name, char** label_keys, char** label_values, int label_count, char* metric_output); #endif ``` ## context using C functions: sprintf, vsprintf, snprintf, vsnprintf ## output Output the complete code for `src/monitoring_system.c` in the following format: ```xml path1 ```
